He is ashamed of his behaviour.  
(Anh ấy xấu hổ về hành vi của mình.) 
jealous/envious of 
ghen tị với 
She is jealous of her sister’s success.  
(Cô ấy ghen tị với thành công của chị gái.) 
suspicious of 
nghi ngờ 
They are suspicious of his motives.  
(Họ nghi ngờ động cơ của anh ấy.) 
tired of 
chán 
I am tired of waiting.  
(Tôi chán việc chờ đợi.) 
full of 
đầy 
The room is full of students.  
(Căn phòng đầy học sinh.) 
short of 
thiếu 
We are short of time.  
(Chúng ta thiếu thời gian.) 
conscious of 
ý thức về 
He is conscious of his mistakes.  
(Anh ấy ý thức được lỗi của mình.) 
guilty of 
có tội về 
He was guilty of fraud.  
(Anh ta có tội lừa đảo.) 
independent of 
độc lập; không còn bị 
ảnh hưởng 
The decision is independent of outside pressure.  
(Quyết định độc lập với áp lực bên ngoài.) 
typical of 
tiêu biểu cho

supportive of

5. Tính từ + from 
Cấu trúc 
Nghĩa 
Ví dụ 
different from 
khác với 
His view is different from mine.  
(Quan điểm của anh ấy khác với tôi.) 
free from 
không có / thoát khỏi

(Nước không bị ô nhiễm.) 
safe from 
an toàn khỏi

(Ngôi làng an toàn khỏi lũ lụt.) 
protected from 
được bảo vệ khỏi

(Da cần được bảo vệ khỏi ánh nắng.) 
isolated from 
bị cô lập khỏi

(Anh ấy cảm thấy bị cô lập khỏi xã hội.) 
removed from 
tách khỏi

separate from

Nghĩa 
Ví dụ

với

involved in 
tham gia vào

(Anh ấy tham gia công tác từ thiện.) 
successful in 
thành công trong 
She was successful in business.  
(Cô ấy thành công trong kinh doanh.) 
experienced in 
có kinh nghiệm trong 
He is experienced in marketing.  
(Anh ấy có kinh nghiệm trong marketing.) 
skilled in/at 
có kỹ năng trong 
She is skilled in negotiation.  
(Cô ấy có kỹ năng đàm phán.) 
deficient in 
thiếu hụt về 
The diet is deficient in vitamins.  
(Chế độ ăn thiếu vitamin.) 
rich in 
giàu về 
The soil is rich in minerals.  
(Đất giàu khoáng chất.) 
poor in 
nghèo về 
The area is poor in natural resources.  
(Khu vực nghèo tài nguyên.)

TÀI LIỆU LIVESTREAM ĐỘC QUYỀN 
VIP 90 (2026): TỔNG ÔN TRỌNG ĐIỂM BỘ CÂU HỎI  
DỄ TRÚNG TỦ & LUYỆN BỘ ĐỀ TINH HOA  
[V90.03.03] LÀM CHỦ KIẾN THỨC NGỮ PHÁP   
 
GIỚI TỪ & CỤM GIỚI TỪ (BUỔI 3) 
Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương  
 
 
CÁC CẤU TRÚC GIỚI TỪ ĐI TRƯỚC DANH TỪ THƯỜNG GẶP 
Cấu trúc 
Nghĩa 
Ví dụ 
by heart 
thuộc lòng 
She learned the poem by heart.  
(Cô ấy học thuộc lòng bài thơ.) 
by chance/ by accident/ 
by mistake 
tình cờ 
I met him by chance.  
(Tôi gặp anh ấy tình cờ.) 
by law 
theo luật 
Helmets are required by law.  
(Theo luật phải đội mũ bảo hiểm.) 
in advance 
trước 
Please inform us in advance.  
(Vui lòng báo trước.) 
in charge (of) 
phụ trách 
She is in charge of the team.  
(Cô ấy phụ trách đội.) 
in demand 
được săn lùng 
Skilled workers are in demand.  
(Lao động tay nghề cao đang được săn đón.) 
in danger 
gặp nguy hiểm 
The species is in danger of extinction.  
(Loài này có nguy cơ tuyệt chủng.) 
in favour of 
ủng hộ 
They voted in favour of the proposal.  
(Họ bỏ phiếu ủng hộ đề xuất.) 
in response to 
đáp lại 
He spoke in response to criticism.  
(Anh ấy phát biểu để đáp lại chỉ trích.) 
in trouble 
gặp rắc rối 
He is in trouble at school.  
(Anh ấy gặp rắc rối ở trường.) 
in debt 
mắc nợ 
Many families are in debt.  
(Nhiều gia đình đang mắc nợ.) 
in control 
kiểm soát 
She remained in control.  
(Cô ấy vẫn kiểm soát được tình hình.) 
in need (of) 
cần 
The victims are in need of help.  
(Các nạn nhân cần được giúp đỡ.) 
in vain 
vô ích 
Their efforts were in vain.  
(Mọi nỗ lực đều vô ích.) 
in effect 
có hiệu lực 
The rule is now in effect.  
(Quy định hiện có hiệu lực.) 
in place 
được thiết lập 
Safety measures are in place.  
(Biện pháp an toàn đã được thiết lập.) 
in practice 
trên thực tế 
The system works in practice.  
(Hệ thống hoạt động trên thực tế.)

Biên soạn: Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương || Độc quyền và duy nhất tại: tienganhcomaiphuong.vn  
 
in theory 
về lý thuyết 
In theory, it seems simple.  
(Về lý thuyết thì có vẻ đơn giản.) 
in reality 
trên thực tế 
In reality, it is difficult.  
(Trên thực tế, nó khó.) 
in detail 
chi tiết 
He explained it in detail.  
(Anh ấy giải thích chi tiết.) 
at risk 
có nguy cơ 
The patient is at risk.  
(Bệnh nhân có nguy cơ.) 
at ease 
thoải mái 
She felt at ease.  
(Cô ấy cảm thấy thoải mái.) 
at fault 
có lỗi 
He was at fault.  
(Anh ấy có lỗi.) 
at stake 
bị đe doạ 
Thousands of jobs are at stake.  
(Hàng nghìn việc làm bị đe doạ.) 
at odds 
bất đồng 
They are at odds over policy.  
(Họ bất đồng về chính sách.) 
on time 
đúng giờ 
The train arrived on time.  
(Tàu đến đúng giờ.) 
on purpose 
cố ý 
She did it on purpose.  
(Cô ấy làm điều đó có chủ ý.) 
on duty 
đang làm nhiệm 
vụ 
The nurse is on duty.  
(Y tá đang trực.) 
on leave 
nghỉ phép 
He is on leave this week.  
(Anh ấy nghỉ phép tuần này.) 
on schedule 
đúng tiến độ 
The project is on schedule.  
(Dự án đúng tiến độ.) 
on average 
trung bình 
On average, students study two hours.  
(Trung bình học sinh học hai giờ.) 
under pressure 
chịu áp lực 
She is under pressure.  
(Cô ấy đang chịu áp lực.) 
under threat 
bị đe doạ 
Wildlife is under threat.  
(Động vật hoang dã đang bị đe doạ.) 
under control 
được kiểm soát 
The fire is under control.  
(Đám cháy đã được kiểm soát.) 
under discussion 
đang được thảo 
luận 
The issue is under discussion.  
(Vấn đề đang được thảo luận.) 
under construction 
đang được xây 
dựng 
The bridge is under construction.  
(Cây cầu đang được xây dựng.) 
out of date 
lỗi thời 
This software is out of date.  
(Phần mềm này lỗi thời.) 
out of work 
thất nghiệp 
Many workers are out of work.  
(Nhiều công nhân thất nghiệp.) 
out of order 
hỏng 
The machine is out of order.  
(Máy bị hỏng.) 
out of control 
mất kiểm soát 
The situation is out of control.

VIP 90PLUS: TỔNG ÔN TRỌNG ĐIỂM & LUYỆN ĐỀ TINH HOA NĂM 2026 
Biên soạn: Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương || Độc quyền và duy nhất tại: tienganhcomaiphuong.vn  
 
(Tình hình mất kiểm soát.) 
out of reach 
ngoài tầm với 
The book is out of reach.  
(Cuốn sách ngoài tầm với.) 
 
CÁC CẤU TRÚC DANH TỪ ĐI TRƯỚC GIỚI TỪ THƯỜNG GẶP 
Cấu trúc 
Nghĩa 
Ví dụ 
demand/need for 
nhu cầu về 
The demand for skilled workers is increasing.  
(Nhu cầu về lao động tay nghề cao đang tăng.) 
lack / shortage of 
sự thiếu hụt 
There is a shortage of clean water in rural areas.  
(Có sự thiếu hụt nước sạch ở vùng nông thôn.) 
reason / explanation 
for 
lý do / lời giải 
thích cho 
She gave no explanation for her absence.  
(Cô ấy không đưa ra lời giải thích cho sự vắng mặt.) 
reaction / response to 
phản ứng với 
His response to the criticism was calm.  
(Phản ứng của anh ấy trước sự chỉ trích rất bình tĩnh.) 
key / solution / 
answer to 
Hard work is the key to success.  
(Chăm chỉ là chìa khóa dẫn đến thành công.) 
chìa khóa / giải 
pháp / câu trả lời 
cho 
increase / rise in 
sự gia tăng 
There has been a sharp rise in prices.  
(Đã có sự gia tăng mạnh về giá cả.) 
decrease/decline/ 
reduction in 
sự giảm 
We noticed a decline in sales.  
(Chúng tôi nhận thấy sự sụt giảm doanh số.) 
impact / effect / 
influence on 
tác động lên 
Technology has a major impact on education.  
(Công nghệ có tác động lớn đến giáo dục.) 
cause of 
nguyên nhân của 
Stress is a major cause of illness.  
(Căng thẳng là nguyên nhân chính của bệnh tật.) 
fear of 
nỗi sợ về 
Fear of failure prevents success.  
(Nỗi sợ thất bại cản trở thành công.) 
awareness of/about 
nhận thức về 
There is growing awareness of climate change.  
(Có sự nhận thức ngày càng tăng về biến đổi khí hậu.) 
evidence / proof of 
bằng chứng của 
There is clear evidence of corruption.  
(Có bằng chứng rõ ràng về tham nhũng.) 
threat / danger to 
mối đe doạ đối 
với 
Pollution poses a threat to wildlife.  
(Ô nhiễm gây mối đe dọa cho động vật hoang dã.) 
damage to 
thiệt hại đối với 
The storm caused serious damage to houses.  
(Cơn bão gây thiệt hại nghiêm trọng cho nhà cửa.) 
contribution to 
sự đóng góp cho 
She made a significant contribution to the project.  
(Cô ấy đã đóng góp đáng kể cho dự án.) 
devotion / 
commitment to 
sự cống hiến /  
cam kết với 
His commitment to the cause is admirable.  
(Sự cam kết của anh ấy với mục tiêu thật đáng ngưỡng mộ.) 
access to 
quyền tiếp cận 
Many citizens lack access to healthcare.  
(Nhiều người dân thiếu quyền tiếp cận dịch vụ y tế.) 
attitude to / towards 
thái độ đối với 
His attitude towards work has changed.  
(Thái độ của anh ấy với công việc đã thay đổi.)

responsibility for 
trách nhiệm về 
She took responsibility for the mistake.  
(Cô ấy nhận trách nhiệm về lỗi đó.) 
progress in 
tiến bộ trong 
She is making progress in mathematics.  
(Cô ấy đang tiến bộ môn toán.) 
experience in 
kinh nghiệm về 
He has experience in marketing.  
(Anh ấy có kinh nghiệm trong marketing.) 
emphasis on 
sự nhấn mạnh 
vào 
The school places emphasis on discipline.  
(Trường chú trọng vào kỷ luật.) 
support for 
sự ủng hộ cho 
There is strong support for reform.  
(Có sự ủng hộ mạnh mẽ cho cải cách.) 
preference for 
sự ưa thích 
She has a preference for classical music.  
(Cô ấy có sự ưa thích nhạc cổ điển.) 
concern about / for/ 
over  
sự lo ngại về 
There is growing concern about pollution.  
(Có sự lo ngại ngày càng tăng về ô nhiễm.) 
 
CÁC CỤM GIỚI TỪ THƯỜNG GẶP 
1. Các cụm chỉ lý do 
Cấu trúc 
Nghĩa 
Ví dụ 
due to/ because of 
do / vì 
The flight was cancelled due to bad weather.  
(Chuyến bay bị hủy do thời tiết xấu.) 
owing to 
do / vì (trang 
trọng hơn) 
Owing to heavy traffic, we arrived late.  
(Do giao thông đông, chúng tôi đến muộn.) 
thanks to 
nhờ có 
Thanks to her help, we finished early.  
(Nhờ sự giúp đỡ của cô ấy, chúng tôi hoàn thành sớm.) 
as a result of 
do kết quả của 
Many species disappeared as a result of climate change. 
(Nhiều loài biến mất do biến đổi khí hậu.) 
on account of 
vì / do  
He was absent on account of illness.  
(Anh ấy vắng mặt vì bệnh.) 
as a consequence of 
do hậu quả của 
Jobs were lost as a consequence of automation.  
(Việc làm bị mất do hậu quả của tự động hóa.) 
in view of 
xét về / do 
In view of recent events, the policy was revised.  
(Xét tình hình gần đây, chính sách đã được sửa đổi.) 
in light of 
dưới góc độ / 
do 
In light of new evidence, the case was reopened.  
(Dựa trên bằng chứng mới, vụ án được mở lại.) 
given 
xét rằng / vì 
Given his experience, he was chosen for the role.  
(Xét kinh nghiệm của anh ấy, anh được chọn.) 
considering 
xét rằng / cân 
nhắc rằng 
Considering the cost, the plan seems reasonable.  
(Xét chi phí, kế hoạch có vẻ hợp lý.) 
out of 
vì (động cơ 
cảm xúc) 
She spoke out of frustration.  
(Cô ấy nói vì sự bực bội.) 
by/in virtue of 
something 
bằng cách/ vì 
She got the job by virtue of her greater experience.  
(Cô ấy có được công việc này nhờ kinh nghiệm dày dặn hơn.)

2. Các cụm chỉ sự nhượng bộ 
Cấu trúc 
Nghĩa 
Ví dụ 
despite/ in spite of 
mặc dù 
Despite the rain, the match continued.  
(Mặc dù trời mưa, trận đấu vẫn tiếp tục.) 
regardless of/ 
irrespective of 
bất kể 
Regardless of the cost, they proceeded with the plan.  
(Bất kể chi phí, họ vẫn tiến hành kế hoạch.) 
notwithstanding 
mặc dù 
Notwithstanding the difficulties, the project succeeded.  
(Mặc dù gặp khó khăn, dự án vẫn thành công.) 
for all 
mặc dù  
For all his wealth, he was unhappy.  
(Mặc dù giàu có, anh ấy không hạnh phúc.) 
 
3. Các cụm khác 
Cấu trúc 
Nghĩa 
Ví dụ 
instead of 
thay vì 
She chose tea instead of coffee.  
(Cô ấy chọn trà thay vì cà phê.) 
thanks to 
nhờ vào (tích cực) 
Thanks to modern medicine, many diseases are curable.  
(Nhờ y học hiện đại, nhiều bệnh có thể chữa được.) 
with regard to 
liên quan đến 
With regard to your request, we will respond soon.  
(Liên quan đến yêu cầu của bạn, chúng tôi sẽ phản hồi sớm.) 
by means of 
bằng cách / bằng 
phương tiện 
The message was sent by means of email.  
(Thông điệp được gửi bằng email.) 
in contrast to 
trái ngược với 
In contrast to his brother, he is very outgoing.  
(Trái với anh trai, anh ấy rất hướng ngoại.) 
in place of 
thay thế cho 
Plastic is used in place of wood.  
(Nhựa được dùng thay thế cho gỗ.) 
in addition to 
ngoài ra / thêm 
vào đó 
In addition to English, she speaks French.  
(Ngoài tiếng Anh, cô ấy còn nói tiếng Pháp.) 
except (for) 
ngoại trừ 
Everyone passed except for one student.  
(Mọi người đều đậu ngoại trừ một học sinh.) 
apart from / aside 
from 
ngoại trừ, ngoài 
Apart from their house in London, they also have a villa in 
Spain.  
(Ngoài căn nhà ở London, họ còn sở hữu một biệt thự ở Tây 
Ban Nha.) 
prior to 
trước khi  
Prior to the meeting, we reviewed the report.  
(Trước cuộc họp, chúng tôi đã xem lại báo cáo.) 
with a view to + 
V-ing 
nhằm mục đích 
They saved money with a view to buying a house.  
(Họ tiết kiệm tiền nhằm mục đích mua nhà.) 
in terms of 
về mặt / xét về 
The project was successful in terms of cost efficiency.  
(Dự án thành công xét về hiệu quả chi phí.)

PRACTICE 
Read the following texts and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct option that best fits 
each of the numbered blanks from 1 to 20.  
Text 1 
We are excited to announce our annual writing contest for students of all levels. Everyone is welcome to 
join, (1) _____ age or experience. Participants are encouraged to share original stories or essays on the 
theme “Dreams and the Future.” Your writing should show how your ideas can make a positive impact 
(2) _____ society. Please submit your entry (3) ______ advance to avoid technical problems. Winners 
will receive prizes and certificates. We look forward to reading your creative work! 
Question 1. A. due to 
B. irrespective of 
C. in terms of 
D. owing to 
Question 2. A. of 
B. to 
C. from 
D. on  
Question 3. A. in 
B. at 
C. with 
D. to 
 
Text 2 
After graduation, many students feel (4) _____ pressure to find a suitable job quickly. The demand (5) 
_____ skilled workers is high in some fields, but competition can still be strong. Graduates should prepare 
a clear CV and practise interview skills carefully. A positive attitude (6) ______ learning and self-
improvement can help them stand out from other candidates. Although the process may seem stressful at 
first, patience and effort often lead to better opportunities in the long run. 
Question 4. A. of 
B. to 
C. under 
D. from 
Question 5. A. in 
B. for 
C. of 
D. at 
Question 6. A. for 
B. from 
C. with 
D. to 
 
Text 3 
Many animals around the world are (7) ______ risk of extinction because of human activities. Pollution, 
hunting, and climate change pose a threat (8) _____ wildlife and their natural habitats. Forests are cut 
down for farming or building houses (9) _____ being protected. As a result, many species lose their homes 
and food sources. In response (10) _____ this serious problem, governments and environmental groups 
are creating laws and wildlife reserves. However, everyone must take responsibility to protect endangered 
animals and support conservation efforts. 
Question 7. A. at 
B. by 
C. with 
D. in 
Question 8. A. for 
B. from 
C. of 
D. to 
Question 9. A. aside from 
B. instead of 
C. on account of 
D. despite 
Question 10. A. at 
B. of 
C. to 
D. from 
 
Text 4 
Plastic pollution is a growing global problem. There is serious concern (11) _____ the amount of plastic 
waste in oceans and rivers. Every year, plastic causes damage (12) _____ marine life and coastal areas. 
Many animals eat plastic by mistake, and their survival is (13) _____ stake. (14) _____ the long time 
plastic needs to break down, the situation may become worse if people do not change their habits. 
Governments and environmental groups are working to raise awareness (15) ______ the dangers of plastic 
use. However, real change depends on individuals reducing waste and choosing reusable products.

VIP 90PLUS: TỔNG ÔN TRỌNG ĐIỂM & LUYỆN ĐỀ TINH HOA NĂM 2026 
Biên soạn: Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương || Độc quyền và duy nhất tại: tienganhcomaiphuong.vn  
 
TÀI LIỆU LIVESTREAM ĐỘC QUYỀN 
VIP 90 (2026): TỔNG ÔN TRỌNG ĐIỂM BỘ CÂU HỎI  
DỄ TRÚNG TỦ & LUYỆN BỘ ĐỀ TINH HOA  
[V90.03.04] TINH HOA ĐỌC HIỂU (BUỔI 5) 
 
 
Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương  
LEVEL 1 
 
 
Text 1 
The rapid growth of artificial intelligence is changing the world of work, especially for white-
collar employees. Many office workers once believed their jobs were safe because they required education 
and professional skills. However, AI tools can now write reports, analyse data and even create designs in 
a short time. As a result, some workers feel uncertain about their future. Instead of waiting to lose their 
jobs, they decide to change careers.   
Some people choose practical jobs that require human contact, such as teaching, healthcare or 
skilled trades. These roles are harder for machines to replace because they depend on communication, 
care and physical work. Others return to school to learn new skills that AI cannot easily copy. Although 
changing careers can be daunting and may mean earning less money at first, many workers say they 
prefer stability and peace of mind. This trend shows how technology is not only transforming industries 
but also influencing personal life choices. 
 
Question 1. The word “they” in paragraph 1 refers to _____. 
A. AI tools 
B. jobs 
C. office workers 
D. skills  
Question 2. AI tools now can do all of the following EXCEPT _____.  
A. offer therapy sessions  
 
B. analyse data efficiently  
C. write detailed reports  
 
D. create professional designs 
Question 3. Which of the following jobs is less likely to be replaced by machines?  
A. Jobs involving digital automation  
B. Jobs involving routine data tasks  
C. Jobs involving human interaction  
D. Jobs involving technical programming 
Question 4. The word “daunting” in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______. 
A. violent 
B. serious 
C. pleasant 
D. excellent 
 
 
Trang 34

TUẦN SỐ 03 
 
VIP 90PLUS: TỔNG ÔN TRỌNG ĐIỂM & LUYỆN ĐỀ TINH HOA NĂM 2026 
Biên soạn: Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương || Độc quyền và duy nhất tại: tienganhcomaiphuong.vn  
 
LEVEL 2 
 
 
Text 2 
Artificial intelligence is transforming the labour market in a powerful way, and experts describe 
this change as similar to a “tsunami” hitting jobs around the world. According to research shared by the 
head of the International Monetary Fund, AI could affect around 60 % of jobs in advanced economies and 
40 % globally in the coming years. Some roles will be improved by AI, but many tasks may be transformed 
or eliminated completely.  
Young people looking for work may be among the most affected groups. Many entry-level 
positions, which often help young people begin their careers, involve simple or routine tasks. These 
tasks are more likely to be automated, making it harder for young job seekers to find good placements 
and gain work experience. As a result, early career opportunities could decrease, and this may slow down 
the development of skills for the next generation of workers.  
At the same time, the rise of AI creates a strong need for new skills in areas like technology and 
problem-solving. Governments and businesses may need to invest in training programmes to help workers 
adapt. If people can learn the skills that employers want, they may benefit from the new jobs that AI also 
helps create.  
 
Question 5. According to research, AI is expected to ______.  
A. improve global salary levels  
B. replace all human workers  
C. reduce workplace competition  
D. influence a large share of jobs 
Question 6. The word “eliminated” in paragraph 1 can be best replaced by ______.  
A. responded 
B. removed 
C. spread 
D. requested  
Question 7. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 2? 
Many entry-level positions, which often help young people begin their careers, involve simple or 
routine tasks. 
A. Many early-career positions that give young people their first step into working life involve basic or 
repetitive tasks.   
B. Repetitive or straightforward tasks often create the early-career positions through which young people 
enter working life.  
C. Young people often shape early-career positions that are formed by routine or basic tasks at the start 
of working life.  
D. Working life for many young people is structured by early-career positions that develop routine or 
straightforward tasks. 
Question 8. According to paragraph 2, automation may reduce opportunities for young people to ______.  
A. develop strong professional networks  
B. secure quality jobs and practical experience  
C. increase their long-term income  
D. receive stable financial support 
Question 9. As stated in paragraph 3, the growth of AI ______.  
A. reduces support for worker training  
B. creates demand for new skills and training  
C. limits access to technical education  
D. removes the need for problem-solving 
Question 10. The word “they” in paragraph 3 refers to ______. 
A. employers 
B. skills 
C. programmes 
D. people 
 
 
 
Trang 35

VIP 90PLUS: TỔNG ÔN TRỌNG ĐIỂM & LUYỆN ĐỀ TINH HOA NĂM 2026 
Biên soạn: Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương || Độc quyền và duy nhất tại: tienganhcomaiphuong.vn  
 
LEVEL 3 
 
 
Text 3 
Many people argue that strict health and safety rules slow down economic progress, especially in 
building infrastructure and major projects. They say excessive regulation makes workplaces more 
expensive and complicated, which can discourage investment and impede growth. However, this view 
has been strongly challenged by experts in occupational safety, who believe that weakening safety 
protections is both dangerous and short-sighted.  
The existing framework of health and safety laws has helped the UK reduce workplace deaths, 
injuries and illnesses over many years. These protections also support long-term productivity by keeping 
workers healthy and reducing the burden on public services. If the government reduces or simplifies these 
standards to speed up development, it could reverse decades of progress and ultimately increase human 
and financial costs.  
Strong regulation does not need to hinder business success. Many believe that well-designed safety 
standards can coexist with economic ambition by creating safe environments where workers feel confident 
and motivated. Instead of cutting protections to chase short-term growth, policymakers are urged to 
maintain robust rules that protect people, sustain reputations and make the country competitive in the 
global market. 
 
Question 11. The word “impede” in paragraph 1 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _____. 
A. delay 
B. promote 
C.  welcome 
D. adjust 
Question 12. According to experts in occupational safety, weakening safety protections is ______.

A. increase pressure on public services

C. create unnecessary legal burdens  
D. support worker health and productivity

A. regulate 
B. discourage 
C. accelerate 
D. supervise  
Question 15. What is implied about chasing short-term growth?  
A. It may lead to weakening important safety protections.  
B. It guarantees faster and safer economic expansion.  
C. It strengthens public trust in national industries.  
D. It improves working conditions for employees.

VIP 90PLUS: TỔNG ÔN TRỌNG ĐIỂM & LUYỆN ĐỀ TINH HOA NĂM 2026 
Biên soạn: Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương || Độc quyền và duy nhất tại: tienganhcomaiphuong.vn  
 
LEVEL 4 
 
 
Text 4 
The steady rise of home working has begun to recalibrate long-standing assumptions about where 
and how professional labour should take place. Advances in broadband infrastructure, cloud computing 
and collaborative platforms have enabled employees to remain fully integrated into organisational 
workflows without occupying a conventional office. [I]  
A significant catalyst behind this transformation is the growing dissatisfaction with lengthy 
commutes and inflexible working routines. Travelling to centralised workplaces often consumes hours 
each day, eroding both productivity and personal well-being. By contrast, home working allows 
individuals to reclaim this time and allocate it more efficiently. For many employers, the arrangement 
also proves economically advantageous, as reduced office occupancy lowers expenditure on rent, 
maintenance and other overheads.  
[II] The appeal of remote work is further reinforced by its potential to enhance autonomy and 
concentration. Many professionals report that a quieter domestic environment enables them to 
undertake cognitively demanding tasks with fewer interruptions than in open-plan offices. 
Moreover, the ability to structure one’s working hours with greater discretion can foster a stronger sense 
of responsibility and engagement with professional duties. [III]  
Nevertheless, the expansion of home working is not without complications. Some employees 
encounter difficulties maintaining clear psychological boundaries between professional obligations and 
private life, while others miss the spontaneous interaction that physical workplaces naturally facilitate. 
[IV] Despite such challenges, the growing normalisation of remote work suggests that it will remain an 
enduring component of contemporary employment practices. 
Question 16. The word “recalibrate” in paragraph 1 mostly means ______. 
A. renovate 
B. reflect 
C. reshape 
D. refurbish 
Question 17. Which of the following best summarises paragraph 2? 
A. Long commutes and rigid office schedules drive home working, saving time and reducing company 
costs.  
B. Many employees prefer home working because it offers greater flexibility and improves productivity.  
C. Companies increasingly promote remote work as it lowers office expenses and modernises workplace 
culture.  
D. Remote work has become popular as employees enjoy flexibility and avoid stressful office 
environments. 
Question 18. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 3? 
Many professionals report that a quieter domestic environment enables them to undertake 
cognitively demanding tasks with fewer interruptions than in open-plan offices. 
A. Many professionals find that the quiet of their home office helps them perform all their daily tasks 
more efficiently than they ever could in a noisy office. 
B. Professionals have stated that a quieter environment at home is necessary for demanding work, though 
open-plan offices are better for simple, routine tasks. 
C. Many employees claim that telecommuting provides a setting with less noise, allowing them to handle 
complex work with more focus than they would have in a shared office space. 
D. A growing number of employees share the notion that a silent home is the place where they can 
complete their most difficult work, as open-plan offices are always too distracting.

VIP 90PLUS: TỔNG ÔN TRỌNG ĐIỂM & LUYỆN ĐỀ TINH HOA NĂM 2026 
Biên soạn: Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương || Độc quyền và duy nhất tại: tienganhcomaiphuong.vn  
 
TÀI LIỆU LIVESTREAM ĐỘC QUYỀN 
VIP 90 (2026): TỔNG ÔN TRỌNG ĐIỂM BỘ CÂU HỎI  
DỄ TRÚNG TỦ & LUYỆN BỘ ĐỀ TINH HOA  
[V90.03.04B] THI ONLINE: TINH HOA ĐỌC HIỂU  
 
(BUỔI 5) – ĐỀ VẬN DỤNG 
Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương  
 
 
LEVEL 1 
Text 1 
Teamwork plays an important role in the workplace. When employees work together, they can share ideas 
and support each other. This helps them solve problems more quickly and effectively. In a team, each 
person has different skills and experiences, so they can learn from one another. Teamwork also improves 
communication because members need to listen and express their opinions clearly. As a result, the 
working environment becomes more positive and friendly. However, teamwork can sometimes be 
challenging if people have different opinions or working styles. Therefore, it is important to respect others 
and cooperate well. In general, good teamwork helps companies achieve their goals and increases overall 
productivity and success. 
Question 1. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in the passage as a benefit of teamwork? 
A. Allow people to learn from each other 
B. Solve problems quickly and effectively 
C. Improve communication  
D. Reduce company costs significantly  
Question 2. When can teamwork become challenging, according to the passage? 
A. When team members have different opinions or working styles 
B. When employees share ideas and support each other 
C. When members communicate clearly with one another 
D. When everyone works toward the same company goals  
Question 3. The word “their” in the text refers to ______. 
A. working styles 
B. companies  
C. goals  
D. others  
 
Text 2 
Online jobs are becoming popular among young people today. Many students choose to work online 
because it is flexible and convenient. One common job is freelance writing, where young people create 
articles or blog posts for websites. Another option is online tutoring, which allows them to teach subjects 
they are good at through video calls. Some young people also work as content creators on social media 
platforms, making videos or sharing useful information. In addition, online shops and businesses often 
hire young workers to manage pages, reply to customers, or design simple posts for their campaigns. 
These online jobs provide income and help young people develop useful skills. 
Question 4. The word “flexible” in the text is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______. 
A. comfortable 
B. annoyed 
C. adaptable 
D. fixed 
Question 5. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in the passage as an online job for young people? 
A. online tutoring 
B. freelance writing 
C. open online shops 
D. content creators  
Question 6. The word “their” in the text refers to _______. 
A. young workers  
 
B. pages 
  
C. online shops and businesses  
D. simple posts

Biên soạn: Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương || Độc quyền và duy nhất tại: tienganhcomaiphuong.vn  
 
LEVEL 2 
Text 3 
Hard skills are specific abilities that people learn through education or training. They are often technical 
skills that can be measured and tested. For example, knowing how to use computer software, speaking a 
foreign language, coding, accounting, or operating machines are hard skills. These skills are important 
because they help employees complete specific tasks and meet job requirements. Employers usually look 
at hard skills when they read a CV or conduct a job interview. Without hard skills, it is difficult for a 
person to perform professional duties effectively. 
In contrast, soft skills are personal qualities that affect how people work and interact with others. They 
include communication, teamwork, problem-solving, time management, and leadership. Soft skills help 
employees cooperate with colleagues and handle challenges in the workplace. Unlike hard skills, soft 
skills are harder to measure but equally important for long-term success. 
Question 7. The word “measured” in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to ______. 
A. exchanged 
B. collected 
C. assessed 
D. absorbed 
Question 8. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? 
Without hard skills, it is difficult for a person to perform professional duties effectively. 
A. Professional duties are easier to complete when workers focus more on soft skills. 
B. Without technical abilities, a person may not carry out job responsibilities efficiently.  
C. Employees can manage their responsibilities well if they lack specialized training. 
D. Hard skills are unnecessary because experience alone guarantees effective performance. 
Question 9. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in the passage as one of the soft skills? 
A. Time management 
 
B. Communication 
 
C. Teamwork 
 
D. Operating machines 
Question 10. According to the passage, soft skills are _______. 
A. as important for long-term success as hard skills 
B. unnecessary if a person has strong qualifications 
C. much more valuable than technical abilities  
D. easy to measure and evaluate through examinations 
 
Text 4 
Many people strongly support the idea that high school students should have part-time jobs while 
they are still studying. They believe that working at a young age helps teenagers develop essential life 
skills such as time management, responsibility, problem-solving, and communication. In addition, earning 
their own money teaches them financial awareness and independence. A part-time job can also help 
students explore different career options and gain practical experience, which may give them an 
advantage when applying for university or future employment. 
On the other hand, many critics argue that high school students should concentrate primarily on 
their academic performance. School years are important for building a strong educational foundation, and 
part-time work may distract students from their studies. Long working hours can cause fatigue, stress, 
and reduced focus in class. Furthermore, teenagers need time for rest, extracurricular activities, and 
social development. Balancing work and study can therefore become overwhelming if not carefully 
managed.

D. Students working extra hours rarely experience stress or problems paying attention in class. 
Question 15. The word “overwhelming” in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to _______. 
A. financial  
B. effortless 
C. ordinary 
D. unmanageable 
 
LEVEL 3 
Text 5 
Youth unemployment remains a pressing issue in Spain, particularly in the aftermath of prolonged 
economic instability and structural labor market weaknesses. Despite gradual recovery following the 
financial crisis and the pandemic, Spain continues to record one of the highest youth unemployment rates 
in the European Union. Many young people struggle to secure stable employment, even after completing 
university degrees or vocational training. Temporary contracts and short-term positions dominate entry-
level opportunities, limiting long-term security and professional growth. 
Several structural factors contribute to this situation. The mismatch between education and labor 
market demands leaves many graduates overqualified for available jobs or lacking the practical skills 
required by employers. [I] Additionally, rigid labor regulations and a dual labor market system tend to 
protect permanent workers while leaving young employees in precarious roles. [II] Economic sectors 
such as tourism, which employ many young people, are also highly sensitive to external shocks. 
[III] Persistent unemployment can lead to financial dependence, delayed family formation, and 
increased emigration among skilled youth. Addressing this challenge requires comprehensive reforms in 
education, labor policy, and investment in innovation-driven industries. [IV]  
Question 16. The word “recovery” in paragraph 1 is CLOSEST in meaning to ______. 
A. success 
B. respect 
C. restoration 
D. contribution 
Question 17. According to paragraph 1, even after completing university degrees or vocational training, 
many young people in Spain _______. 
A. still struggle to secure stable employment 
B. obtain temporary  contracts with companies 
C. achieve growth in entry-level positions 
D. no longer face challenges in the market

VIP 90PLUS: TỔNG ÔN TRỌNG ĐIỂM & LUYỆN ĐỀ TINH HOA NĂM 2026

TÀI LIỆU LIVESTREAM ĐỘC QUYỀN 
VIP 90 (2026): TỔNG ÔN TRỌNG ĐIỂM BỘ CÂU HỎI  
DỄ TRÚNG TỦ & LUYỆN BỘ ĐỀ TINH HOA  
[V90.03.04A] THI ONLINE: TINH HOA ĐỌC HIỂU  
 
(BUỔI 5) – ĐỀ NÂNG CAO 
Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương  
 
 
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of 
the questions from 1 to 8. 
The COVID-19 pandemic did not simply relocate work from offices to living rooms; it triggered 
a profound reassessment of career identity. As millions transitioned to remote arrangements almost 
overnight, many white-collar employees confronted an unexpected question: if work can be done from 
anywhere, what exactly anchors it to a particular employer or city? The collapse of routine commuting 
exposed how much time and emotional energy had been absorbed by pre-pandemic norms, prompting 
workers to scrutinise whether those sacrifices were justified. 
For some, remote work illuminated longstanding frustrations. Without the social pressure of the 
office, dissatisfaction with rigid hierarchies, limited advancement or misaligned values became harder to 
ignore. The blurring of domestic and professional spheres also intensified burnout, especially for parents 
and caregivers managing parallel responsibilities. Yet this strain coexisted with a new sense of autonomy. 
Employees gained visibility into alternative possibilities — freelance work, relocation, career pivots — 
that once felt impractical. 
At the same time, labour shortages in certain sectors shifted bargaining power towards workers. 
Companies competing for talent expanded flexible policies, raised compensation or reconsidered 
geographic pay structures. This environment emboldened many to resign, retrain or renegotiate their roles 
rather than accept pre-crisis conditions. 
Ultimately, the pandemic functioned as a catalyst rather than a cause. It accelerated latent 
dissatisfaction and widened awareness of choice. The result is not merely a rise in job switching, but a 
deeper cultural shift in how individuals define security, fulfilment and professional agency in an 
increasingly fluid labour market. 
 
Question 1. The word “it” in paragraph 1 refers to ______.  
A. question 
B. energy 
C. work 
D. pandemic 
Question 2. The word “burnout” in paragraph 2 can be best replaced by _____. 
A. disconnection 
B. exhaustion 
C. weakness 
D. sensitivity 
Question 3. The word “emboldened” in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______. 
A. disallowed 
B. refused 
C. discouraged 
D. reduced 
Question 4. Which of the following is NOT mentioned as an alternative possibility that once felt 
impractical?  
A. career pivots  
 
B. entrepreneurship  
 
C. relocation  
 
D. freelance work 
 
 
 
 
Trang 43

Question 5. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 4? 
It accelerated latent dissatisfaction and widened awareness of choice. 
A. The pandemic sped up underlying frustration and expanded people’s sense of available options.  
B. Underlying frustration hastened the pandemic and broadened its range of possible outcomes.  
C. A wider sense of available options intensified the pandemic and deepened existing frustration.   
D. Existing frustration and greater awareness of options advanced the pandemic’s development. 
Question 6. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?  
A. Office culture strengthened employees’ attachment to traditional commuting routines.  
B. Labour shortages reduced workers’ leverage in negotiating pay and flexibility.  
C. Remote work prompted many employees to reassess their career identity.  
D. The pandemic created dissatisfaction that had never existed before. 
Question 7. Which paragraph mentions greater independence during remote work? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4 
Question 8. Which paragraph mentions workers questioning the value of past routines? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4 
 
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of 
the questions from 9 to 18. 
The transition from university to the labour market has become increasingly fraught for newly 
minted graduates. Despite holding degrees and often having completed internships, many young 
adults are encountering prolonged periods of unemployment and underemployment. [I] In 
interviews with CNN, graduates report submitting hundreds of applications without receiving meaningful 
responses, underscoring the disconnect between academic preparation and the realities of the 
contemporary job market. This phenomenon has been compounded by heightened competition, as degree 
attainment has soared while entry-level opportunities have not expanded proportionately.  
A confluence of structural factors is contributing to these challenges. Employers frequently 
emphasise work experience and practical skills over formal credentials alone, meaning that even well-
qualified candidates may be overlooked if they lack specific industry exposure. Hiring managers and 
economists have noted that the expectations for entry-level positions have risen, effectively making the 
labour market more selective. Furthermore, macroeconomic conditions and industry shifts have led some 
organisations to delay or reduce graduate hiring, leaving many young workers in a precarious position.   
[II] Graduates often contend with mounting student loan obligations, cost-of-living pressures, and 
the psychological toll of repeated rejection. Some individuals are resorting to temporary or unrelated work 
to make ends meet, compromising their long-term career trajectories. [III] This misalignment between 
graduate aspirations and job availability raises questions about the efficacy of current higher education 
models, particularly how well they equip students for rapidly evolving employment landscapes.   
Despite these obstacles, there are emerging responses from both institutions and students. 
Universities are increasingly expanding career support services, integrating experiential learning 
opportunities and stronger employer partnerships in an effort to bridge the gap between graduates and 
meaningful employment. [IV] Simultaneously, graduates themselves are recalibrating expectations and 
developing new strategies — such as broadening their skill sets, pursuing further education, or exploring 
non-traditional career paths — to enhance their adaptability in an uncertain economic climate.

Question 14. Why do some graduates have to do temporary or unrelated work?  
A. Because of financial strain and repeated rejection.  
B. Because employers demand extra certifications.  
C. Because short-term jobs offer rapid promotion.

TUẦN SỐ 03 
 
VIP 90PLUS: TỔNG ÔN TRỌNG ĐIỂM & LUYỆN ĐỀ TINH HOA NĂM 2026 
Biên soạn: Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương || Độc quyền và duy nhất tại: tienganhcomaiphuong.vn  
 
TÀI LIỆU LIVESTREAM ĐỘC QUYỀN 
VIP 90 (2026): TỔNG ÔN TRỌNG ĐIỂM BỘ CÂU HỎI  
DỄ TRÚNG TỦ & LUYỆN BỘ ĐỀ TINH HOA  
[V90.03.05] DỰ ĐOÁN ĐẶC BIỆT CỤM ĐỘNG TỪ    
 
XUẤT HIỆN TRONG ĐỀ THI (BUỔI 1) 
Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương  
 
 
Cụm động từ với break 
Cụm động từ 
Nghĩa 
Ví dụ 
break down 
hỏng; suy sụp; phân 
hủy 
The machine broke down during production.  
(Cái máy bị hỏng trong lúc sản xuất.) 
break out 
bùng phát 
A fire broke out in the factory last night.  
(Một vụ cháy đã bùng phát trong nhà máy tối qua.) 
break up 
chia tay; giải tán 
Their marriage has broken up. (Họ đã ly hôn.) 
break in/ into 
đột nhập 
Thieves broke into the warehouse.  
(Kẻ trộm đã đột nhập vào nhà kho.) 
break through 
đột phá 
Scientists think they are beginning to break through in the 
fight against cancer.  
(Các nhà khoa học tin rằng họ đang bắt đầu có những bước 
đột phá trong cuộc chiến chống ung thư.) 
break off 
chấm dứt đột ngột 
They broke off negotiations after the dispute.  
(Họ chấm dứt đàm phán sau cuộc tranh cãi.) 
break with 
đoạn tuyệt; từ bỏ 
The company broke with tradition and changed its policy.  
(Công ty đã từ bỏ truyền thống và thay đổi chính sách.) 
 
Cụm động từ với bring 
Cụm động từ 
Nghĩa 
Ví dụ 
bring about 
gây ra; dẫn đến 
The reform brought about significant changes.  
(Cuộc cải cách đã dẫn đến những thay đổi đáng kể.) 
bring up 
nuôi dưỡng; đề cập 
She was brought up in a rural area.  
(Cô ấy được nuôi dưỡng ở vùng nông thôn.) 
bring in 
đưa ra; thu được; 
mời/thuê 
The new policy brought in higher revenue.  
(Chính sách mới đã mang lại doanh thu cao hơn.) 
bring out 
làm nổi bật; xuất bản 
The report brings out serious concerns.  
(Báo cáo làm nổi bật những lo ngại nghiêm trọng.) 
bring back 
mang trở lại; gợi nhớ 
The song brings back childhood memories.  
(Bài hát gợi lại ký ức tuổi thơ.) 
bring forward 
dời lên; đề xuất để 
thảo luận 
The meeting was brought forward to Monday.  
(Cuộc họp được dời lên thứ Hai.) 
 
Trang 47

VIP 90PLUS: TỔNG ÔN TRỌNG ĐIỂM & LUYỆN ĐỀ TINH HOA NĂM 2026 
Biên soạn: Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương || Độc quyền và duy nhất tại: tienganhcomaiphuong.vn  
 
bring down 
hạ xuống; lật đổ 
The scandal brought down the government.  
(Vụ bê bối đã làm chính phủ sụp đổ.) 
bring off 
đạt được (khó khăn) 
The team brought off a surprising victory.  
(Đội đã giành được chiến thắng đầy bất ngờ.) 
bring along 
mang theo; đưa đi 
cùng 
She brought her sister along to the event.  
(Cô ấy đưa em gái theo đến sự kiện.) 
bring on 
gây ra; làm xuất hiện 
Stress can bring on headaches.  
(Căng thẳng có thể gây ra đau đầu.) 
 
Cụm động từ với come 
Cụm động từ 
Nghĩa 
Ví dụ 
come about 
xảy ra 
The change came about unexpectedly.  
(Sự thay đổi xảy ra một cách bất ngờ.) 
come across 
tình cờ gặp; tạo ấn 
tượng; được hiểu 
I came across an old friend yesterday.  
(Hôm qua tôi tình cờ gặp một người bạn cũ.) 
come up with 
nghĩ ra; đề xuất 
She came up with a brilliant idea.  
(Cô ấy nghĩ ra một ý tưởng tuyệt vời.) 
come up 
xảy ra; được nhắc 
đến 
The issue came up during the meeting.  
(Vấn đề được nhắc đến trong cuộc họp.) 
come out 
xuất bản; lộ ra 
The truth finally came out.  
(Sự thật cuối cùng cũng được tiết lộ.) 
come along 
tiến triển; đi cùng 
The project is coming along well.  
(Dự án đang tiến triển tốt.) 
come into 
thừa kế 
She came into a large fortune.  
(Cô ấy được thừa kế một gia tài lớn.) 
come over 
ghé thăm 
Why don’t you come over tonight?  
(Tối nay bạn ghé qua nhé?) 
come down to 
rốt cuộc là; quy về 
It comes down to personal choice.  
(Rốt cuộc là do lựa chọn cá nhân.) 
come in for 
hứng chịu  
The company came in for heavy criticism after the 
scandal.  
(Công ty đã nhận nhiều chỉ trích sau vụ bê bối.) 
come down with 
đổ bệnh 
She came down with the flu last week.  
(Tuần trước cô ấy bị cúm.) 
come round 
hồi tỉnh; thay đổi 
quan điểm, tâm trạng 
He fainted but soon came round.  
(Anh ấy ngất đi nhưng nhanh chóng tỉnh lại.)

Cụm động từ với get 
Cụm động từ 
Nghĩa 
Ví dụ 
get along (with) 
hòa hợp với 
She gets along well with her colleagues.  
(Cô ấy hòa hợp tốt với đồng nghiệp.) 
get over 
vượt qua 
He finally got over the disappointment.  
(Cuối cùng anh ấy cũng vượt qua được sự thất vọng.) 
get through 
vượt qua; hoàn thành 
She got through the exam successfully.  
(Cô ấy đã vượt qua kỳ thi thành công.) 
get by 
xoay xở; sống tạm 
They can get by on a small income.  
(Họ có thể xoay xở với thu nhập thấp.) 
get across 
truyền đạt rõ 
He struggled to get his point across.  
(Anh ấy gặp khó khăn khi truyền đạt quan điểm.) 
get ahead 
thăng tiến 
She works hard to get ahead in her career.  
(Cô ấy làm việc chăm chỉ để thăng tiến.) 
get away (with) 
thoát tội; thoát khỏi 
He got away with cheating.  
(Anh ta đã thoát khỏi việc bị phạt vì gian lận.) 
get down to 
bắt tay vào 
It’s time to get down to work.  
(Đã đến lúc bắt tay vào làm việc.) 
get rid of 
loại bỏ 
They got rid of old equipment.  
(Họ đã loại bỏ thiết bị cũ.) 
get through to 
liên lạc được 
I couldn’t get through to him.  
(Tôi không thể liên lạc được với anh ấy.)

medicine. After a few days, I (8) _____ and started feeling better. This illness (9) _____ a lesson for me 
- I realised how important it is to rest and take care of my health. Now, I always try to eat well and sleep 
enough.

Read the following questions and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct option that best 
fits each of the numbered blanks from 1 to 20. 
Question 1. The sudden policy shift ______ widespread debate across academic circles. 
A. came across 
B. got by 
C. brought about 
D. broke off 
Question 2. After hours of discussion, the committee decided to ______ the proposal for further review. 
A. break down 
B. bring forward 
C. get over 
D. come up

A. bring in  
B. bring about  
C. break down  
D. come about  
Question 4. She struggled to ______ her complex theory during the conference presentation. 
A. break with 
B. bring down 
C. get across 
D. come round

C. get over 
D. break off

B. break down 
C. bring about 
D. come across

A. come about 
B. brought in 
C. broken off 
D. gotten over 
Question 9. She managed to ______ a viable solution despite limited resources. 
A. break with 
B. bring down 
C. come up with 
D. get through 
Question 10. The organisation decided to ______ outdated procedures. 
A. break through 
B. come about 
C. bring in 
D. get rid of 
Question 11. Despite financial constraints, the startup managed to ______ on minimal funding. 
A. break down 
B. come up 
C. bring about 
D. get by 
Question 12. The scholar attempted to ______ traditional methodology in her research. 
A. get ahead 
B. break with 
C. come round 
D. bring along 
Question 13. Unexpected technical difficulties caused the system to ______ during testing. 
A. bring forward 
B. break down 
C. get over 
D. come about 
Question 14. Over time, the innovative approach began to ______ in the competitive market. 
A. get rid of 
B. bring off 
C. break through 
D. come down with 
Question 15. He eventually ______ to her perspective after reviewing the evidence. 
A. broke off 
B. brought about 
C. came round 
D. got by 
Question 16. The publication is expected to ______ next month. 
A. get ahead 
B. come out 
C. break down 
D. bring up

TUẦN SỐ 03 
 
VIP 90PLUS: TỔNG ÔN TRỌNG ĐIỂM & LUYỆN ĐỀ TINH HOA NĂM 2026 
Biên soạn: Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương || Độc quyền và duy nhất tại: tienganhcomaiphuong.vn  
 
TÀI LIỆU LIVESTREAM ĐỘC QUYỀN 
VIP 90 (2026): TỔNG ÔN TRỌNG ĐIỂM BỘ CÂU HỎI  
DỄ TRÚNG TỦ & LUYỆN BỘ ĐỀ TINH HOA  
[V90.03.06] TINH HOA ĐỌC HIỂU (BUỔI 6) 
 
 
Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương  
LEVEL 1 
 
 
Text 1 
In recent years, Mexico City has become a popular place for remote workers from the United 
States. According to a report by CNN, many Americans moved there after the pandemic because they 
could keep their jobs while working online. Compared to large US cities, the cost of living in Mexico City 
is lower, and the city offers warm weather, rich culture, and a lively social life.  
Neighbourhoods such as Roma and Condesa have become especially popular. These areas are 
known for their cafés, parks, and beautiful streets. Foreign workers often choose to rent apartments there 
because they feel comfortable and modern.  
However, this change has also created problems. As more foreigners arrive, rent prices have 
soared. Some local residents say it is now harder to afford housing in their own city. While new businesses 
benefit from foreign spending, many locals worry about rising costs and changes to their communities. 
Mexico City shows how remote work can reshape a city in both positive and challenging ways. 
 
Question 1. Mexico City offers all of the following EXCEPT ______.  
A. warm weather  
 
B. rich culture  
  
C. a peaceful rural lifestyle  
D. a lively social life 
Question 2. The word “they” in paragraph 2 refers to _____. 
A. neighbourhoods 
B. foreign workers 
C. apartments 
D. areas  
Question 3. The word “soared” in paragraph 3 mostly means ______. 
A. declined gradually 
 
B. risen quickly 
C. remained stable 
 
D. become affordable 
Question 4. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 3? 
Mexico City shows how remote work can reshape a city in both positive and challenging ways. 
A. Mexico City illustrates that remote employment can transform an urban area in both beneficial and 
difficult respects.  
B. Remote employment demonstrates how Mexico City can alter work patterns in ways that are 
advantageous yet demanding.  
C. Mexico City demonstrates that urban environments have the capacity to reshape remote employment 
in constructive and complex respects.  
D. Remote employment in Mexico City reveals how urban centres respond positively and critically to 
changing conditions. 
 
 
 
 
 
Trang 57

VIP 90PLUS: TỔNG ÔN TRỌNG ĐIỂM & LUYỆN ĐỀ TINH HOA NĂM 2026 
Biên soạn: Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương || Độc quyền và duy nhất tại: tienganhcomaiphuong.vn  
 
LEVEL 2 
 
 
Text 2 
In 2022, a new idea called ‘quiet quitting’ became a big topic online, especially on TikTok. The 
phrase does not mean that someone leaves their job completely. Instead, it means workers do only what 
their job requires and do not do extra tasks like staying late or helping without being paid extra. This idea 
reached many people because one short video about it got millions of views, and the hashtag 
#QuietQuitting was used millions of times on social media.  
People who support quiet quitting say it helps them find a better work-life balance and avoid stress 
or burnout. They feel that work should not take over their whole life, and they want time for their personal 
interests, family, or rest. Some workers say this concept lets them focus on what they are actually paid to 
do and keeps them from feeling overwhelmed.  
However, others think quiet quitting could make some employers feel workers are not interested 
or committed, and this might affect teamwork or future opportunities. The discussion has shown that many 
people today want clearer boundaries between work and life while still doing their jobs responsibly. 
 
Question 5. Quiet quitting means _______.  
A. leaving a position without giving any formal notice in advance  
B. doing only the duties required in a job and not going beyond them  
C. refusing to cooperate with colleagues during working hours  
D. searching for alternative employment while remaining in the same role 
Question 6. The word “it” in paragraph 1 refers to ______. 
A. job 
B. hashtag 
C. TikTok 
D. quiet quitting 
Question 7. People that support quiet quitting want time for all of the following EXCEPT ______.  
A. rest  
 
B. personal interests  
 
C. family 
 
D. professional training 
Question 8. The word “overwhelmed” in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _____. 
A. pressured 
B. brave 
C. relaxed 
D. unpleasant 
Question 9. The discussion over quiet quitting shows that ______.  
A. workers avoid effort to reduce pressure  
 
B. workers seek balance without neglecting duties  
C. workers reject commitment for comfort  
D. workers prefer comfort over responsibility

VIP 90PLUS: TỔNG ÔN TRỌNG ĐIỂM & LUYỆN ĐỀ TINH HOA NĂM 2026 
Biên soạn: Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương || Độc quyền và duy nhất tại: tienganhcomaiphuong.vn  
 
LEVEL 3 
 
 
Text 3 
In a reflective essay titled ‘Stay true to yourself – and fly closer to the sun,’ writer Bob Brody 
explains what he has learned from 50 years of rejection in his career. Over decades, he has sent 
thousands of ideas to editors, only to receive far more ‘no’ than ‘yes.’ At times, he experienced shock, 
anger and self-doubt, especially in his younger years. However, Brody eventually learned to accept 
rejection as part of creative work and life.  
Brody points out that some rejections happen for reasons outside his control — such as editorial 
needs or timing — and others because his work simply did not fit. Rather than seeing rejection as proof 
of failure, he now views it as a chance to improve his ideas. He learned to analyse his pitches carefully, 
revise them where necessary, and submit them again.  
Despite the thousands of setbacks, Brody has published more than 1,000 essays and two books. 
He admits that he has not won major prizes or achieved fame, but he finds satisfaction in his long career. 
Ultimately, his experience taught him to value resilience and stay true to his own voice, even when faced 
with repeated disappointment. 
 
Question 10. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? 
Over decades, he has sent thousands of ideas to editors, only to receive far more ‘no’ than ‘yes.’ 
A. Over time, he has obtained a greater number of approvals than rejections from those reviewing his 
submissions.  
B. Across his career, editors have granted him more positive responses than negative ones for his 
suggestions.  
C. Throughout the years, his proposals to publishers have been met with many more refusals than 
acceptances. 
D. For decades, his submissions have brought him a higher total of favourable replies than dismissals. 
Question 11. Brody sometimes experienced all of the following EXCEPT ______.  
A. shock 
B. gratitude  
C. self-doubt  
D. anger 
Question 12. The word “it” in paragraph 2 refers to _____.  
A. proof 
B. control 
C. failure 
D. rejection 
Question 13. What is indicated about the rejections Brody received?   
A. They may reflect personal shortcomings.  
B. They may signal limited ability.  
C. They may result from external factors.  
D. They may reveal flawed judgement. 
Question 14. What is implied in the last paragraph? 
A. Career success depends mainly on external approval.  
B. Lasting satisfaction requires widespread admiration.  
C. Personal fulfilment can matter more than public recognition. 
D. External recognition can determine long-term career satisfaction. 
 
 
 
 
 
Trang 59

VIP 90PLUS: TỔNG ÔN TRỌNG ĐIỂM & LUYỆN ĐỀ TINH HOA NĂM 2026 
Biên soạn: Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương || Độc quyền và duy nhất tại: tienganhcomaiphuong.vn  
 
LEVEL 4 
 
 
Text 4 
The boundary between work and leisure has become increasingly blurred in modern life. 
Sociologist Dalton Conley introduced the term ‘weisure’ to describe this phenomenon, a blend of ‘work’ 
and ‘leisure’ referring to the way professional responsibilities increasingly seep into periods once reserved 
for free time. [I] 
Weisure does not arise solely because mobile phones or email allow employers to contact workers 
outside office hours. It also reflects a deeper social shift. [II] Compared with previous generations, people 
are now more likely to form close friendships through work and less likely to spend their careers within 
large, stable organisations that once maintained clearer boundaries between professional and private life. 
Activities such as networking further blur the distinction between social relationships and 
professional advancement. 
Common advice for dealing with this problem usually focuses on protecting leisure from work. 
People are encouraged to switch off their phones or delay email responses so that work cannot intrude so 
easily into personal time. Yet this perspective overlooks another dimension of the issue: the role of leisure 
itself in encouraging the spread of work into everyday life. 
In modern society, leisure is often defined simply as the absence of work – the time left over after 
earning a living. [III] Because it is perceived as empty or unstructured, it can easily become colonised by 
work-related activity. Ironically, research on psychological ‘flow’ suggests that work frequently provides 
more focus and engagement than many leisure activities, such as watching television. As a result, the 
boundary between work and leisure continues to dissolve. [IV] 
 
Question 15. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 2? 
Activities such as networking further blur the distinction between social relationships and 
professional advancement. 
A. Activities like networking increasingly blur the line separating social connections from career 
advancement.  
B. The blurred line between social connections and career advancement encourages people to engage in 
networking activities.  
C. Networking activities cause social relationships to become more important than professional 
advancement.  
D. Professional ambitions lead individuals to develop social relationships through networking 
opportunities. 
Question 16. Which of the following best summarises paragraph 3? 
A. Advice often suggests limiting digital communication outside work hours to protect leisure time.  
B. Many people manage work–life balance by ignoring emails and switching off phones after work.  
C. Advice protects leisure from work intrusion yet overlooks how leisure itself encourages work’s spread.  
D. Modern advice focuses on protecting personal time from digital technology and constant 
communication. 
Question 17. The word “colonised” in paragraph 4 mostly means ______.  
A. reflected 
B. regulated 
C. dominated 
D. confined 
 
 
 
Trang 60

[V90.03.06B] THI ONLINE: TINH HOA ĐỌC HIỂU  
 
(BUỔI 6) – ĐỀ VẬN DỤNG 
Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương  
 
 
LEVEL 1 
Text 1 
Technology has a strong impact on jobs in modern society. On the one hand, technology creates many 
new job opportunities. For example, people can work in IT, digital marketing, or online services. 
Technology also helps workers complete tasks faster and more efficiently. Machines and computers can 
do difficult or dangerous work, which makes workplaces safer. On the other hand, technology can also 
cause job loss. Some traditional jobs are replaced by machines or automated systems. As a result, some 
workers may lose their jobs and need to learn new skills. In general, technology changes the job market, 
bringing both benefits and challenges to employees and employers. 
 
Question 1. The word “impact” in the text can be best replaced by ______. 
A. knowledge 
B. goal 
C. effect 
D. price  
Question 2. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in the passage as a benefit of technology? 
A. Making workplaces safer 
 
B. Creating new job opportunities 
C. Helping complete tasks efficiently 
D. Reducing the need to learn skills  
Question 3. The word “their” in the text refers to ______. 
A. machines  
 
B. some workers  
 
C. jobs  
 
D. automated systems 
 
Text 2 
Work-life balance is the ability to divide time and energy between work and personal life in a healthy 
way. It means having enough time for family, friends, hobbies, and rest while still completing work 
responsibilities. When people have a good work-life balance, they feel less stressed and more satisfied 
with their lives. However, today, many people are losing this balance. Modern technology allows 
employees to check emails and work at any time, even at home. Long working hours and high pressure 
also make it difficult to relax. As a result, people may feel tired, anxious, or disconnected from their loved 
ones. This situation is becoming more common in many countries. 
 
Question 4. The word “responsibilities” in the text is CLOSEST in meaning to _______. 
A. duties 
B. emotions 
C. standards 
D. employees 
Question 5. According to the text, when people have a good work-life balance, they _______. 
A. avoid communicating with family and friends 
B. spend all their time focusing on work responsibilities 
C. are unable to complete their professional duties 
D. feel less stressed and more satisfied with their lives  
 
 
Trang 62

LEVEL 2 
Text 3 
Artificial Intelligence is transforming the future of work in many significant ways. AI systems can 
analyze data, automate repetitive tasks, and improve decision-making processes. In industries such as 
finance, healthcare, and manufacturing, AI increases efficiency and reduces human error. As a result, 
companies can lower costs and improve productivity. Moreover, AI is creating new types of jobs, 
including data analysts, AI engineers, and digital strategy specialists. These changes require workers to 
develop advanced technical skills and adapt to new technologies. 
However, AI also raises concerns about job displacement. Many routine or low-skilled positions may 
disappear as machines become more capable. This shift could increase inequality if workers do not 
receive proper training. Therefore, education systems and organizations must focus on reskilling and 
lifelong learning. The future of work will likely depend on how effectively humans and AI collaborate 
rather than compete.

D. digital strategy specialists

Many routine or low-skilled positions may disappear as machines become more capable. 
A. Routine workers will keep their positions because technology cannot fully replace them. 
B. As machines grow more advanced, lots of repetitive or low-skilled jobs could be eliminated.  
C. Low-skilled employees are certain to receive promotions when automation increases. 
D. The improvement of machines guarantees the creation of more manual labor opportunities. 
Question 10. What does the author predict about the future of work? 
A. It will depend on how effectively humans and AI collaborate. 
B. Humans will be replaced by AI systems in most industries. 
C. All low-skilled jobs will disappear without any alternatives. 
D. Technology will disappear once firms have reduced costs.

Text 4 
Following one’s passion means choosing a career based on personal interests and strong 
motivation rather than financial security. Many people believe that when individuals love what they do, 
they are more creative, productive, and satisfied with their lives. Passion-driven careers often encourage 
innovation and personal growth, especially in fields such as art, entrepreneurship, or research. However, 
these paths may involve financial uncertainty, unstable income, or intense competition, which can create 
stress and long-term pressure. 
In contrast, choosing a stable job focuses on security, steady income, and clear career progression. 
Stable careers, such as in public service, education, or established companies, often provide benefits, 
predictable salaries, and long-term protection. While such jobs may sometimes feel less exciting, they 
reduce financial risk and offer peace of mind. Ultimately, the decision depends on personal priorities, 
risk tolerance, and individual definitions of success.

Text 5 
Blue-collar jobs typically involve manual labor, technical skills, or physical tasks, often performed 
in industries such as construction, manufacturing, transportation, or maintenance. These occupations 
usually require vocational training or practical apprenticeships rather than advanced academic degrees. 
Blue-collar workers play a fundamental role in sustaining infrastructure and production systems, ensuring 
that societies function efficiently. Although these jobs have sometimes been undervalued socially, they 
often provide stable income and strong demand, particularly in economies facing skilled labor shortages. 
In contrast, white-collar jobs are generally associated with office-based, administrative, or 
professional work. They commonly require higher education qualifications and emphasize cognitive skills 
such as analysis, communication, and strategic thinking. Fields such as finance, law, education, 
management, and information technology fall into this category. White-collar roles are often perceived 
as offering higher social status and clearer pathways for career advancement. However, they may 
also involve high levels of stress and intense competition. 
Despite their differences, the distinction between blue-collar and white-collar employment is 
increasingly blurred. Technological advancement has introduced automation into manual labor while 
simultaneously demanding technical expertise in both sectors. Ultimately, both forms of work are 
essential, interdependent, and valuable within a modern economy.

C. People believe white-collar work always guarantees wealth and no stress. 
D. White-collar employees receive recognition or career development opportunities.

A. improved 
B. disregarded

B. Blue-collar jobs always require advanced university degrees from workers.

D. Both blue-collar and white-collar jobs are essential in a modern economy.

(BUỔI 6) – ĐỀ NÂNG CAO 
Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương  
 
 
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of 
the questions from 1 to 8. 
A recent survey has revealed that one in three workers in the United Kingdom reported calling in 
sick after drinking at work-related events or after socialising with colleagues. The research, conducted by 
the Institute for Public Policy Research (IPPR) and based on a representative sample of over 2,000 adults, 
highlights how alcohol consumption linked to professional life can lead to significant absences from work, 
even if people do not have serious health problems. 
The figures show that around 31% of the workforce have taken a sick day after drinking with 
workmates in the past year, and 22% reported working while still feeling the effects of alcohol. Younger 
employees were among the most affected: about 43% of workers aged 18–24 have called in sick because 
of drinking at or after work events. The survey also found that a notable share of workers feel pressure to 
drink in order to fit in or progress at work, suggesting that workplace culture still strongly associates 
socialising with alcohol.  
Experts say these patterns have consequences beyond individual employees. The report describes 
the issue as a productivity crisis, arguing that alcohol-related absences and reduced capacity while at work 
can compromise overall performance across sectors. Workers who drank heavily were far more likely to 
be present but not fully effective in their roles, a phenomenon known as presenteeism, which also affects 
productivity.   
The IPPR report also points to a gap in employer action. More than half of respondents said 
their workplace had provided no guidance or inclusive alternatives to alcohol-centred events, despite most 
workers believing employers should take responsibility for reducing alcohol harm. Some companies, 
however, have begun offering social activities that do not revolve around drinking, such as exercise 
classes, creative workshops or team-building events that include everyone. 
 
Question 1. According to the research by IPPR, _____  
A. drinking connected to work can result in notable workplace absences.  
B. alcohol consumption rarely causes serious long-term medical conditions.  
C. most employee absences are linked to underlying health problems.  
D. workplace social events have limited influence on staff attendance. 
Question 2. The phrase “fit in” in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _____. 
A. maintain harmony 
B. accept risks 
C. be excluded 
D. share a view  
Question 3. The word “compromise” in paragraph 3 mostly means ______. 
A. adjust 
B. enhance 
C. promote 
D. harm 
Question 4. The word “their” in paragraph 3 refers to _____. 
A. sectors 
B. workers 
C. patterns 
D. consequences

Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of 
the questions from 9 to 18. 
A new global report by the British Standards Institution (BSI) has highlighted a growing concern 
that Generation Z workers are facing a potential ‘job-pocalypse’ as companies increasingly prioritise 
artificial intelligence (AI) over hiring new staff. According to the BSI study of more than 850 business 
leaders across seven countries, including the UK, US and France, many firms are choosing to deploy AI 
tools to fill skills gaps rather than recruit and train junior employees. Nearly four in ten bosses reported 
that AI has already enabled them to reduce their headcount, and almost a third now consider AI solutions 
before even contemplating new hires.   
[I] One quarter of the executives surveyed said they believe that most or all entry-level tasks could 
be fully automated, effectively rendering traditional junior positions obsolete. Around 39 % of leaders 
indicated that junior roles have already been reduced or eliminated as AI takes on administrative, research 
and briefing tasks. This trend suggests that young workers, many of whom are just beginning their careers, 
may struggle to gain essential early experience in the workplace.  
The implications of these shifts extend beyond immediate hiring practices. [II] Experts fear that 
as firms streamline operations with AI, the traditional talent pipeline may weaken, leaving a generation 
of young professionals without the opportunities to develop foundational skills. With unemployment in 
some countries already at multi-year highs and wage growth slowing, the broader labour market 
outlook appears uncertain. Although some economists caution against directly attributing all job losses 
to AI adoption, there is growing unease that the rapid integration of automation could compound long-
term generational inequality and challenge economic stability. [III] 
In response to these concerns, BSI’s chief executive has emphasised the need for a balanced 
approach that values both technological innovation and human contribution. [IV] While AI offers 
undeniable benefits in terms of productivity and efficiency, sustainable employment depends on continued 
investment in people as well as machines. This includes upskilling, retraining and creating pathways for 
young workers to thrive alongside evolving technologies. Employers, educators and policymakers are 
being urged to collaborate in order to ensure that the next generation is not left behind in an AI-driven 
world.

TÀI LIỆU LIVESTREAM ĐỘC QUYỀN 
VIP 90 (2026): TỔNG ÔN TRỌNG ĐIỂM BỘ CÂU HỎI  
DỄ TRÚNG TỦ & LUYỆN BỘ ĐỀ TINH HOA  
[V90.ĐH.03] ĐỌC HIỂU C1 – C2 BỘ ĐỀ DỄ TRÚNG TỦ 
 
(BUỔI 3) 
Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương  
 
 
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of 
the questions from 1 to 8. 
AI Interviews: Business and Practice 
One company leading the way in AI interviewing is HireVue. Founded in 2004, in Salt Lake City, 
Utah, the company provides a complete AI assessment service for companies looking to hire new staff. 
As of 2021, over 700 companies use HireVue, and the company has interviewed over twelve million 
applicants on their behalf. 
A major corporation now using HireVue systems is Unilever, the parent company of familiar food 
brands like Hellmann’s, Knorr, and Lipton tea. Unilever has a combined staff of more than 155,000 
worldwide, and Leena Nair, its head of human resources, has said that using HireVue saves the company 
more than 100,000 hours and $1 million every year. 
But not everyone is convinced that HireVue’s model is fair or effective. A prominent AI researcher 
in the US has called HireVue’s system “pseudoscience,” and a campaigning group, the Electronic Privacy 
Information Center, even sent an official complaint to the Federal Trade Commission. They claimed that 
the way HireVue’s AI systems worked was “unfair, deceptive, biased, and unprovable.” 
In response, Loren Larsen, head of technology at HireVue, has said that during a typical thirty-
minute interview, the company’s AI system collects 500,000 pieces of data about an applicant—far more 
than any human might manage. Larsen was less clear about exactly what data the system records or how 
it interprets this information. 
For applicants, then, there remains the question of how to succeed when taking an AI interview. 
The internet is full of tips and suggestions, which include sitting in front of a blank background, talking 
more slowly than normal, smiling often, looking directly at one’s webcam, and repeating words that 
appear in the job specification. However, as AI interviews do not provide feedback for applicants, for 
now, these ideas are mostly guesswork. 
(Adapted from Reading for the Real World 3) 
 
Question 1. The word “Founded” in paragraph 1 can be best replaced by _______. 
A. Demolished 
B. Removed 
C. Invested 
D. Established 
Question 2. The word “its” in paragraph 2 refers to ________. 
A. Leena Nair 
B. Unilever  
C. a combined staff  
D. Lipton tea 
Question 3. According to paragraph 3, a campaigning group described HireVue’s system as all of the 
following EXCEPT _______. 
A. unprovable 
B. unfair 
C. deceptive 
D. unbiased 
Question 4. The word “typical” in paragraph 4 is opposite in meaning to _______.  
A. normal 
B. indirect 
C. unusual 
D. active 
 
 
Trang 70

the questions from 9 to 18. 
For decades, gender has shaped workforce structures, with men occupying higher-paid, leadership 
roles while women often performed service-based jobs. In Louise Howe's Pink Collar Workers: Inside 
the World of Women's Work, she describes how, despite requiring significant skill and responsibility, 
pink-collar jobs frequently pay less than male-dominated occupations with similar education and 
experience requirements. One reason is the persistent cultural belief that women's work - especially 
caregiving, education, and administrative support - is an extension of their expected domestic 
responsibilities. This assumption undervalues the work done in these roles, keeping wages low and 
limiting career progression. 
Sociologist Christine Williams introduced the concept of the "Glass Escalator" to describe how 
men in female-dominated fields are often fast-tracked to leadership roles. Unlike the well-known "Glass 
Ceiling," which prevents women from rising in male-dominated professions, the Glass Escalator benefits 
men by propelling them into managerial positions in pink-collar sectors. For example, in nursing, a field 
traditionally dominated by women, male nurses are disproportionately promoted to supervisory roles. The 
same trend is evident in education, where male teachers are more likely to become principals. This creates 
an additional barrier for women, as leadership roles remain elusive even within professions they dominate 
numerically. 
Despite requiring comparable education and responsibilities, jobs dominated by women 
consistently pay less than those dominated by men. [I] The Bureau of Labor Statistics provides striking 
examples. Information Technology (IT) managers, a male-dominated profession, earn a median salary 
that is 27% higher than Human Resources (HR) managers, a profession primarily made up of women. [II] 
Janitors, who are typically men, earn 22% more than maids and housecleaners, who are mostly women, 
despite performing similar labor-intensive work. 
 
Trang 71
